perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement, "Confidential Information" means information disclosed by (or on behalf of) one party to the other party under this Agreement that is marked as confidential or, from its nature, content or the circumstances in which it is disclosed, might reasonably be supposed to be confidential, including the terms and conditions (including the Exhibits) of this Agreement. It does not include information that the recipient already knew, that becomes public through no fault of the recipient, that was independently developed by the recipient or that was lawfully given to the recipient by a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement, "Confidential Information" means information disclosed by (or on behalf of) one party to the other party under this Agreement that is marked as confidential or, from its nature, content or the circumstances in which it is disclosed, might reasonably be supposed to be confidential. It does not include information that the recipient already knew, that becomes public through no fault of the recipient, that was independently developed by the recipient or that was lawfully given to the recipient by a third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'including the terms and conditions (including the Exhibits) of this Agreement' from the definition of 'Confidential Information,' it creates ambiguity as to whether the terms and conditions of the agreement are actually confidential. This contradicts the intended purpose of maintaining confidentiality and could lead to disputes over what information is protected. The contradiction appears most directly in section 11.1 where it refers to the confidentiality, as now we don't know if the terms in the agreement are indeed confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to clause 6 (Confidential Information), neither party may make any public statement regarding the relationship contemplated by this Agreement without the other's prior written approval.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly included 'the terms and conditions (including the Exhibits) of this Agreement' within the definition of Confidential Information. The changed text removes this explicit inclusion. The contradicted text, 'Subject to clause 6 (Confidential Information), neither party may make any public statement regarding the relationship contemplated by this Agreement without the other's prior written approval,' relies on clause 6 defining what is confidential. By removing the explicit inclusion of the agreement's terms within the definition of 'Confidential Information,' it creates ambiguity whether the terms of the agreement are confidential, directly contradicting the expectation in the contradicted section that Confidential Information, as defined, is subject to a confidentiality restriction.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor will comply with all applicable commercial and public anti-bribery laws, including, without limitation, the UK's Bribery Act 2010 and the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977 ("Anti-Bribery Laws"), which prohibit (amongst other things) corrupt offers of anything of value, either directly or indirectly, to a government official to obtain or keep business. ''Government officials" include any government employee, candidate for public office, and employee of government-owned or government-controlled companies, public international organisations, and political parties. Furthermore, Distributor will not make any facilitation payments, which are payments to induce officials to perform routine functions they are otherwise obligated to perform.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will comply with some applicable commercial and public anti-bribery laws, including, without limitation, the UK's Bribery Act 2010 and the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977 ("Anti-Bribery Laws"), which prohibit (amongst other things) corrupt offers of anything of value, either directly or indirectly, to a government official to obtain or keep business. ''Government officials" include any government employee, candidate for public office, and employee of government-owned or government-controlled companies, public international organisations, and political parties. Furthermore, Distributor will make facilitation payments, which are payments to induce officials to perform routine functions they are otherwise obligated to perform.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'all applicable' to 'some applicable' and 'will not make' to 'will make' creates a direct contradiction. It initially states compliance with Anti-Bribery Laws, which prohibit facilitation payments, but then it states the Distributor *will* make facilitation payments. This directly contradicts the purpose of complying with the Anti-Bribery Laws as defined. This directly contradicts section 10.4 (a) where it needs to comply with clause 8.5 Anti-Bribery Laws. Here the change in the text, is in direct contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor [ * ] and will indemnify Google against all liabilities, costs, damages and expenses (including settlement costs approved in writing by Distributor and reasonable legal fees [ * ]) suffered or incurred by Google or any Google Group Company arising from: (a) Distributor's improper (ie not in accordance with the requirements of this Agreement including the Exhibits) or unauthorised, replication, packaging, marketing, distribution, or installation of the Products, including any breach of Clause 8.5 and any claims based on representations, warranties, or misrepresentations made by Distributor
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states compliance with 'all applicable' anti-bribery laws and prohibits facilitation payments. The changed text narrows the compliance to 'some applicable' laws and states the distributor 'will make' facilitation payments. This directly contradicts the clause requiring compliance with Anti-Bribery Laws, which prohibits such payments, and therefore it is a strong contradiction to the Contradicted Text that highlights the Distributor will indemnify Google against any breach of clause 8.5 (Anti-Bribery Laws).

perturbation.original_text: All payments under this Agreement shall be made in [ * ] in the [ * ] following the [ * ] for which the payments are applicable. The party receiving payment will be responsible for any bank charges assessed by the recipient's bank. In addition to other rights and remedies Google may have, Google may offset any payment obligations to Distributor that Google may incur under this Agreement against any product or service fees owed to Google and not yet paid by Distributor under any agreement between Distributor and Google. Google may also withhold and offset against its payment obligations under this Agreement, or require Distributor to pay to Google within [ * ] of any invoice, any amounts Google [ * ] overpaid to Distributor in prior periods.
perturbation.changed_text: All payments under this Agreement shall be made in [ * ] in the [ * ] following the [ * ] for which the payments are applicable. The party receiving payment will be responsible for any bank charges assessed by the recipient's bank. In addition to other rights and remedies Google may have, Google may not offset any payment obligations to Distributor that Google may incur under this Agreement against any product or service fees owed to Google and not yet paid by Distributor under any agreement between Distributor and Google. Google may also withhold and offset against its payment obligations under this Agreement, or require Distributor to pay to Google within [ * ] of any invoice, any amounts Google [ * ] overpaid to Distributor in prior periods.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'may offset' to 'may not offset', the provision about Google's remedies becomes contradictory. The original sentence gives Google the right to offset payments, while the new wording explicitly denies this right, while the sentence after that one, states that google CAN offset payment, creating an in-text contradiction. This creates uncertainty in the payment terms and how Google can recover debts from the distributor. The section 4.5, about the interest is directly affected by this contradictory term, now if Google offsets or not, is something to be questioned due to this insertion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor may charge interest at the rate of [ * ] above the base rate of Barclays Bank PLC from time to time, from the due date until the date of actual payment, whether before or after judgment, on any payment pursuant to this Clause 4 (Payment Terms) which is overdue.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'may offset' to 'may not offset' directly contradicts the subsequent sentence in the changed text: 'Google may also withhold and offset against its payment obligations...'. This creates an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text regarding interest payments is directly impacted because the uncertainty surrounding Google's ability to offset payments affects the calculation and applicability of interest charges on overdue payments, as it is unclear when a payment is truly overdue due to the potential for offset.
